verdict_reasoning: SRC is a non-receptor tyrosine kinase canonically anchored to the cytoplasmic (inner) leaflet of the plasma membrane via N-terminal myristoylation, so baseline localization is inner-leaflet/intracellular and not accessible from outside. However, there is documented ecto-SRC / surface-exposed SRC kinase activity on tumor cells and reports of SRC family kinase surface display in transformed / activated contexts, fitting cancer-state ecto-presentation. This qualifies as contextual/cell_state_induced rather than no. Pre-no checks: cancer ecto-pool documented (cannot rule out); no narrow germline lineage display; not lysosomal TM; no canonical PM-luminal pool; no covalent TM-partner tethering.
predicted_key_uncertainty: Strength and reproducibility of ecto-SRC surface display on tumor cells is limited; baseline is firmly inner-leaflet myristoylated and most therapeutics target the intracellular kinase pocket.
